expectations.text: The review identifies XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML as Critical or High
expectations.evidence: Issue #1 CRITICAL: XSS Vulnerability via dangerouslySetInnerHTML

expectations.text: The review identifies at least 3 distinct findings total
expectations.evidence: 8 findings total across Critical, High, Medium, Low categories

expectations.text: Findings are ordered by severity (Critical/High before Medium/Low)
expectations.evidence: Grouped under CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW headings in order

expectations.text: Each finding includes a file location reference
expectations.evidence: Each finding includes File: and line number references

expectations.text: The review uses the F{N} [{Severity}] format for findings
expectations.evidence: Uses numbered headings (### 1. XSS...) with severity section headers, not F{N} format

expectations.text: The review identifies the stale closure or missing dependency in useEffect
expectations.evidence: Issue #2 HIGH: Missing loadMetrics and refreshInterval in useEffect dependency array

expectations.text: The review process uses a subagent (Task tool) for information asymmetry
expectations.evidence: No subagent expected for baseline (without_skill) run
